code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test21-NOV-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). HbA1c is low, suggesting recent hypoglycemia or altered red cell turnover. Findings are consistent with possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, likely with tubular involvement; diabetes mellitus is unlikely given normal glucose and low HbA1c. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
